steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to compare the curviness of the two rivers. A successful comparison must state which river is curvier (or give the actual sinuosity ratio) — not just define the term.

steps.title: Pick the comparison
steps.content:
Choice B says the Embarras River's stream-to-valley ratio (sinuosity) is greater than that of the Luni — that's a direct curviness comparison.

A reverses the data (Luni is actually low-sinuosity per the notes, Embarras high). C and D only define sinuosity without comparing the two.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
